content: Grief is a natural response to loss, particularly death of loved one, characterized by sadness, yearning, guilt, anger, anxiety, somatic symptoms, preoccupation with deceased. Normal grief varies culturally, typically involves waves of emotion ('pangs of grief'), preserved self-esteem, ability to experience positive emotions when reminded of deceased. Complicated grief (Prolonged Grief Disorder) involves persistent, disabling grief >6-12 months with intense yearning, preoccupation, functional impairment. Major Depression can be triggered by loss but differs: pervasive low mood not tied to thoughts of deceased, worthlessness/guilt beyond actions related to loss, suicidal ideation beyond wish to join deceased, psychomotor retardation, prolonged functional impairment. Distinguishing is clinically important as treatment differs.
